key_pointers.record_type_enum: engine/wal/record.go: RecordType (RecordCatalogPut=1, RecordCatalogDelete=2, RecordBTreeInsert=3, RecordBTreeDelete=4; 0 reserved as RecordTypeInvalid)
key_pointers.payload_types: engine/wal/record.go: CatalogPutPayload, CatalogDeletePayload, BTreeInsertPayload, BTreeDeletePayload (+ New*Record constructors and As*() decode accessors)
key_pointers.fsync_before_apply_entry_point: engine/wal/record.go: AppendAndApply(w *Writer, rec TypedRecord, apply func() error) (offset int64, err error) -- calls Writer.Append (1.3.1, fsyncs before returning) THEN apply(), enforced structurally not just documented
key_pointers.ordering_proof_test: engine/wal/record_test.go: TestFsyncBeforeApply -- apply callback independently calls ReadSegment on the WAL dir and asserts the just-written record is already durable/CRC-valid at the moment apply fires
key_pointers.apply_error_semantics_test: engine/wal/record_test.go: TestAppendAndApplyErrorFromApply -- apply() error is propagated (wrapped) but the WAL record remains durably on disk
key_pointers.append_failure_test: engine/wal/record_test.go: TestAppendAndApplyWriterErrorSkipsApply -- Writer.Append failure means apply is never invoked

downstream_notes_for_1.3.3_and_1.3.4: 1.3.4 recovery replay should decode segment records via ReadSegment (1.3.1) + DecodeTypedRecord (this subtask), dispatch on RecordType, and use the As*() accessors to get typed payloads to redo catalog.Put/Delete or btree.Insert/Delete.

engine/wal intentionally does not import engine/catalog or engine/btree -- CatalogPutPayload.Record is an opaque pre-encoded catalog.CatalogRecord blob; decoding it into catalog.CatalogRecord and invoking the real mutation functions belongs to the wiring layer that calls AppendAndApply (not yet built; likely part of 1.3.4 or a later catalog/btree integration subtask).
